from src.core.vocabulary.corpus_manager import get_corpus_manager
from src.core.vocabulary import get_corpus_registry
from src.config import CORPUS_MIN_DOCUMENTS

print\(''=== Corpus Status Check ===''\)
print\(\)

# Check corpus registry
registry = get_corpus_registry\(\)
active = registry.get_active_corpus\(\)
corpora = registry.list_corpora\(\)

print\(f''Active corpus: {active}''\)
print\(f''Total corpora: {len\(corpora\)}''\)
print\(\)

for corpus in corpora:
    status = ''\(ACTIVE\)'' if corpus.name == active else ''''
    print\(f''  {corpus.name}: {corpus.doc_count} documents {status}''\)

print\(\)

# Check BM25 activation
manager = get_corpus_manager\(\)
doc_count = manager.get_document_count\(\)
is_ready = manager.is_corpus_ready\(min_docs=CORPUS_MIN_DOCUMENTS\)

print\(f''Corpus document count: {doc_count}''\)
print\(f''Minimum required: {CORPUS_MIN_DOCUMENTS}''\)
print\(f''BM25 would be enabled: {is_ready}''\)
print\(\)

if not is_ready:
    print\(f''>>> BM25 is DISABLED because you have {doc_count}/{CORPUS_MIN_DOCUMENTS} documents''\)
    print\(f''>>> Add {CORPUS_MIN_DOCUMENTS - doc_count} more documents to enable BM25''\)
else:
    print\(''>>> BM25 should be ENABLED''\)
")

Bash("c:\\Users\\noahc\\Dropbox\\Not Work\\Data Science\\CaseSummarizer\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe" -c "
from src.core.vocabulary import VocabularyExtractor

extractor = VocabularyExtractor\(\)
test_text = ''Dr. John Smith examined the patient. John Smith recommended surgery. Later, John Smith reviewed the results.''

# Run all algorithms
all_results = []
for alg in extractor.algorithms:
    if not alg.enabled:
        continue
    print\(f''{alg.name}:''\)
    result = alg.extract\(test_text\)
    print\(f''  Candidates: {len\(result.candidates\)}''\)
    for c in result.candidates:
        print\(f''    {c.term} | freq={c.frequency} | type={c.suggested_type}''\)
    all_results.append\(result\)

# Merge all
merged = extractor.merger.merge\(all_results\)
print\(\)
print\(f''After merge: {len\(merged\)} terms''\)
for m in merged:
    print\(f''  {m.term} | freq={m.frequency} | type={m.final_type} | sources={m.sources}''\)
")

extractor = VocabularyExtractor\(\)
test_text = ''Dr. John Smith examined the patient. John Smith recommended surgery. Later, John Smith reviewed the results.''

# Run all algorithms
all_results = []
for alg in extractor.algorithms:
    if not alg.enabled:
        continue
    result = alg.extract\(test_text\)

# Merge

print\(f''After post-process: {len\(vocab\)} terms''\)

from src.core.vocabulary import VocabularyExtractor

extractor = VocabularyExtractor\(\)
test_text = ''Dr. John Smith examined the patient. John Smith recommended surgery. Later, John Smith reviewed the results.''

# Run all algorithms
all_results = []
for alg in extractor.algorithms:
    if not alg.enabled:
        continue
    result = alg.extract\(test_text\)
    all_results.append\(result\)

# Merge
merged = extractor.merger.merge\(all_results\)
print\(f''After merge: {len\(merged\)}''\)

# Post-process
vocab = extractor._post_process\(merged, test_text, 1, 100.0\)
print\(f''After post-process: {len\(vocab\)}''\)

# 4. Name deduplication
from src.core.vocabulary.name_deduplicator import deduplicate_names
vocab = deduplicate_names\(vocab\)
print\(f''After dedupe names: {len\(vocab\)}''\)

# 5. Artifact filter
from src.core.vocabulary.artifact_filter import filter_substring_artifacts
vocab = filter_substring_artifacts\(vocab\)
print\(f''After artifact filter: {len\(vocab\)}''\)
for v in vocab:
    print\(f''  {v[""Term""]}''\)

# 5b. Name regularization - THIS IS WHERE IT GETS FILTERED
from src.core.vocabulary.name_regularizer import regularize_names
vocab2 = regularize_names\(vocab\)
print\(f''After name regularization: {len\(vocab2\)}''\)
for v in vocab2:
    print\(f''  {v[""Term""]}''\)
")

import sys
import os
os.environ[''DEBUG''] = ''true''
sys.path.insert\(0, ''.''\)

print\(''=== Q&A Debug Test ===''\)
print\(\)

test_text = ''''''
SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - COUNTY OF KINGS
JOHN SMITH, Plaintiff, versus ACME HOSPITAL and DR. ROBERT JONES, Defendants.
Index No. 12345/2024

Plaintiff JOHN SMITH alleges that on January 15, 2024, he presented to Acme Hospital 
emergency department with severe cervical radiculopathy and bilateral lumbar stenosis.
Defendant DR. ROBERT JONES negligently failed to order MRI and CT imaging.
As a result, plaintiff suffered progressive myelopathy requiring emergency laminectomy.
Plaintiff seeks damages exceeding 500000 dollars.
''''''

from src.services import QAService

print\(''Building Q&A index...''\)
qa_service = QAService\(\)

def progress_cb\(msg\):
    print\(f''  {msg}''\)

success = qa_service.build_index\(test_text, progress_callback=progress_cb\)
print\(f''Build success: {success}''\)
print\(f''Is ready: {qa_service.is_ready}''\)
print\(\)

if qa_service.is_ready:
    print\(''Asking question...''\)
    result = qa_service.ask_question\(''Who is the plaintiff?''\)
    print\(f''Result type: {type\(result\)}''\)
    if result:
        print\(f''Answer: {result.answer if hasattr\(result, ""answer""\) else result}''\)
    else:
        print\(''Result is None''\)
")

Bash("c:\\Users\\noahc\\Dropbox\\Not Work\\Data Science\\CaseSummarizer\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe" -c "
import sys
import os
os.environ[''DEBUG''] = ''true''
sys.path.insert\(0, ''.''\)

print\(''=== Q&A System Test \(Fixed\) ===''\)
print\(\)

test_text = ''''''
SUPREME COURT OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK - COUNTY OF KINGS
JOHN SMITH, Plaintiff, versus ACME HOSPITAL and DR. ROBERT JONES, Defendants.
Index No. 12345/2024

Plaintiff JOHN SMITH alleges that on January 15, 2024, he presented to Acme Hospital 
emergency department with severe cervical radiculopathy and bilateral lumbar stenosis.
Defendant DR. ROBERT JONES negligently failed to order MRI and CT imaging.
As a result, plaintiff suffered progressive myelopathy requiring emergency laminectomy.
Plaintiff seeks damages exceeding 500000 dollars.
''''''

from src.services import QAService

print\(''Building Q&A index...''\)
qa_service = QAService\(\)

def progress_cb\(msg\):
    print\(f''  {msg}''\)

success = qa_service.build_index\(test_text, progress_callback=progress_cb\)
print\(f''Build success: {success}''\)
print\(f''Is ready: {qa_service.is_ready}''\)
print\(\)

if qa_service.is_ready:
    questions = [
        ''Who is the plaintiff?'',
        ''What hospital is named in this case?'',
    ]
    
    for q in questions:
        print\(f''Q: {q}''\)
        result = qa_service.ask_question\(q\)
        if result:
            answer = result.answer if hasattr\(result, ''answer''\) else str\(result\)
            if len\(answer\) > 200:
                answer = answer[:200] + ''...''
            print\(f''A: {answer}''\)
        else:
            print\(''A: \(No result\)''\)
        print\(\)
else:
    print\(''Q&A service not ready - cannot ask questions''\)

print\(''=== Test Complete ===''\)
")

    {''Term'': ''the same'', ''Is Person'': ''No'', ''In-Case Freq'': 3},

]

Bash("c:\\Users\\noahc\\Dropbox\\Not Work\\Data Science\\CaseSummarizer\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe" -c "
from src.core.vocabulary.filters import \(
    create_default_filter_chain,
    create_optimized_filter_chain,
\)

# Test vocabulary with various term types
test_vocab = [
    {''Term'': ''John Smith'', ''Is Person'': ''Yes'', ''In-Case Freq'': 10},
    {''Term'': ''radiculopathy'', ''Is Person'': ''No'', ''In-Case Freq'': 5},
    {''Term'': ''the same'', ''Is Person'': ''No'', ''In-Case Freq'': 3},
    {''Term'': ''cervical stenosis'', ''Is Person'': ''No'', ''In-Case Freq'': 4},
    {''Term'': ''xkjwqr'', ''Is Person'': ''No'', ''In-Case Freq'': 1},  # gibberish
    {''Term'': ''Dr. Robert Jones'', ''Is Person'': ''Yes'', ''In-Case Freq'': 8},
]

# Run default chain \(6 separate filters\)
default_chain = create_default_filter_chain\(\)
default_result = default_chain.run\(test_vocab.copy\(\)\)

# Run optimized chain \(combined per-term filter\)
optimized_chain = create_optimized_filter_chain\(\)
optimized_result = optimized_chain.run\(test_vocab.copy\(\)\)

print\(''=== Default Chain \(6 filters\) ===''\)
print\(f''Filters: {len\(default_chain.filters\)}''\)
print\(f''Removed: {default_result.removed_count}''\)
print\(f''Remaining: {[t[""Term""] for t in default_result.vocabulary]}''\)
print\(\)

print\(''=== Optimized Chain \(4 filters\) ===''\)
print\(f''Filters: {len\(optimized_chain.filters\)}''\)
print\(f''Removed: {optimized_result.removed_count}''\)
print\(f''Remaining: {[t[""Term""] for t in optimized_result.vocabulary]}''\)
print\(\)

# Compare results
default_terms = set\(t[''Term''] for t in default_result.vocabulary\)
optimized_terms = set\(t[''Term''] for t in optimized_result.vocabulary\)

if default_terms == optimized_terms:
    print\(''Results MATCH - both chains produce identical vocabulary''\)
else:
    print\(''Results DIFFER!''\)
    print\(f''Only in default: {default_terms - optimized_terms}''\)
    print\(f''Only in optimized: {optimized_terms - default_terms}''\)
")

Bash("c:\\Users\\noahc\\Dropbox\\Not Work\\Data Science\\CaseSummarizer\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe" -c "
from src.core.vocabulary.algorithms.rake_algorithm import RAKEAlgorithm
from src.core.vocabulary.name_regularizer import regularize_names, _term_validity_score

# Test 1: RAKE title case fix
print\(''=== Test 1: RAKE Title Case ===''\)
rake = RAKEAlgorithm\(\)
test_phrase = ''luigi napolitano''
cleaned = rake._clean_phrase\(test_phrase\)
print\(f''Input: ""{test_phrase}""''\)
print\(f''Output: ""{cleaned}""''\)
assert cleaned == ''Luigi Napolitano'', f''Expected ""Luigi Napolitano"", got ""{cleaned}""''
print\(''PASS: Names now use title case''\)
print\(\)

# Test 2: Validity scoring
print\(''=== Test 2: Validity Scoring ===''\)
# Correct spelling should score higher than typo
correct = ''Smith''
typo = ''Smitb''  # OCR error - b instead of h
correct_score = _term_validity_score\(correct\)
typo_score = _term_validity_score\(typo\)
print\(f''""{correct}"" validity score: {correct_score}''\)
print\(f''""{typo}"" validity score: {typo_score}''\)
assert correct_score > typo_score, ''Correct spelling should have higher validity score''
print\(''PASS: Correct spelling scores higher''\)
print\(\)

# Test 3: Typo filter keeps correct variant
print\(''=== Test 3: Typo Filter Keeps Correct Variant ===''\)
# Simulate case where typo has HIGHER frequency than correct
vocab = [
    {''Term'': ''Smitb'', ''Is Person'': ''Yes'', ''In-Case Freq'': 10, ''Quality Score'': 50},  # typo, high freq
    {''Term'': ''Smith'', ''Is Person'': ''Yes'', ''In-Case Freq'': 3, ''Quality Score'': 50},   # correct, low freq
]
result = regularize_names\(vocab\)
remaining = [v[''Term''] for v in result]
print\(f''Input: {[v[""Term""] for v in vocab]}''\)
print\(f''Output: {remaining}''\)

# The correct spelling \(Smith\) should be kept, not the typo \(Smitb\)
if ''Smith'' in remaining and ''Smitb'' not in remaining:
    print\(''PASS: Correct spelling kept despite lower frequency''\)
elif ''Smitb'' in remaining and ''Smith'' not in remaining:
    print\(''FAIL: Typo was kept instead of correct spelling''\)
else:
    print\(f''NOTE: Both kept \(may not be close enough for typo detection\)''\)

print\(\)
print\(''=== All Tests Complete ===''\)
")

    {''Term'': ''Arthur Jenkins'', ''Is Person'': ''Yes'', ''In-Case Freq'': 3},   # correct, low freq
]

Bash("c:\\Users\\noahc\\Dropbox\\Not Work\\Data Science\\CaseSummarizer\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe" -c "
from src.core.utils.gibberish_filter import is_gibberish

print\(''=== Testing Updated Gibberish Filter ===''\)
print\(\)

test_cases = [
    \(''the'', False, ''dict word''\),
    \(''plaintiff'', False, ''dict word''\),
    \(''teh'', False, ''short typo''\),
    \(''recieve'', False, ''common typo''\),
    \(''Jenidns'', False, ''name typo''\),
    \(''plaintif'', False, ''legal typo''\),
    \(''cervikal'', False, ''medical typo''\),
    \(''definately'', False, ''spelling error''\),
    \(''xkjwqr'', True, ''random garbage''\),
    \(''asdfgh'', True, ''keyboard mash''\),
    \(''modmess'', True, ''PDF garbage''\),
    \(''quanny'', True, ''PDF garbage''\),
    \(''ModMess Quanny'', True, ''PDF garbage phrase''\),
    \(''Smitb'', False, ''OCR of Smith''\),
]

correct = 0
for word, expected, desc in test_cases:
    result = is_gibberish\(word\)
    match = ''OK'' if result == expected else ''WRONG''
    if result == expected:
        correct += 1
    exp_str = ''gib'' if expected else ''valid''
    got_str = ''gib'' if result else ''valid''
    print\(f''{word:<20} {desc:<18} exp={exp_str:<5} got={got_str:<5} {match}''\)

print\(\)
print\(f''Accuracy: {correct}/{len\(test_cases\)} \({100*correct/len\(test_cases\):.0f}%\)''\)
")
